rubric_metadata.rubric_name: Week 1: Automation Auditor – Intent, Context & Hooks
rubric_metadata.grading_target: Week 1 agent repository (intent tracking, hooks, orchestration)

dimensions.name: Intent–AST Correlation
dimensions.description: How well the system produces a machine-readable trace that maps high-level intents to concrete code changes (AST/content hashes).
dimensions.score_1: No machine-readable trace. Relies only on standard Git history; cannot correlate intents to specific code shapes.
dimensions.score_3: A trace file exists but classification is noisy or ad-hoc. Mapping between intents and code changes is partially correct but often inaccurate.
dimensions.score_5: agent_trace.jsonl (or equivalent) rigorously maps Intent IDs to content hashes / AST nodes. Cleanly distinguishes refactors from features using deterministic logic.

dimensions.name: Context Engineering
dimensions.description: How effectively the agent uses structured context to drive behavior without drifting or overloading prompts.
dimensions.score_1: State/context files are handwritten and static. The agent often drifts from the source of truth; no reliable link between context and behavior.
dimensions.score_3: Hooks or scripts update state/context, but the architecture is brittle. Context is sometimes stale or over-broad; still mostly prompt-driven.
dimensions.score_5: Dynamic injection of an active_intents.yaml (or equivalent). The agent cannot act without referencing this context DB. Context is curated, filtered, and precise rather than a raw dump.

dimensions.name: Hook Architecture
dimensions.description: How cleanly hooks are designed, isolated, and composed around the main execution flow.
dimensions.score_1: Hook logic is stuffed directly into the main execution loop (spaghetti). Cross-cutting concerns are interleaved with core logic.
dimensions.score_3: Hooks exist and work but are tightly coupled to the host application. Limited composability; failures can easily break the main flow.
dimensions.score_5: Clean middleware/interceptor pattern. Hooks are isolated from business logic, composable, and fail-safe (errors are contained and observable).

dimensions.name: Orchestration & Parallelism
dimensions.description: How well the system orchestrates concurrent work without collisions, using shared configuration as a coordination layer.
dimensions.score_1: Single-threaded or single-agent only. No orchestration beyond simple sequential steps.
dimensions.score_3: Attempts at parallelism exist but collide (race conditions, shared state clobbering). No robust coordination primitives.
dimensions.score_5: Parallel orchestration is demonstrated. Shared coordination artifacts (e.g., CLAUDE.md / central contract) prevent collisions. The system behaves as a 'Hive Mind' rather than independent, conflicting workers.

synthesis_rules.fact_priority: Prefer concrete artifacts (trace files, hook wiring, orchestration code) over narrative descriptions when scoring.
synthesis_rules.minimum_viable_trace: If no machine-readable trace exists, cap the overall score at 3 regardless of other dimensions.
synthesis_rules.orchestration_guardrail: If parallel orchestration repeatedly causes collisions, the Orchestration dimension cannot exceed 3 even if other aspects are strong.
